question: Felix Protocol FDV above $300M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Felix's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Felix Protocol doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will there be at least 2000 measles cases in the U.S. in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026 according to the CDC case counter between January 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

events.title: Measles cases in U.S. in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026 according to the CDC case counter between January 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Declining U.S. MMR vaccination rates below the 95% herd immunity threshold—now at 92.7% for kindergarteners per CDC's latest survey—drive trader pessimism for elevated measles cases in 2026, echoing 2024's surge of 248 confirmed cases, the highest since 2019. Authoritative CDC data highlights outbreaks in unvaccinated pockets like religious communities and via international importations, with the virus's R0 of 12-18 amplifying spread risks. While elimination status holds, post-COVID vax hesitancy persists; upcoming annual kindergarten coverage reports this fall and WHO global surveillance updates could shift market-implied odds if immunity rebounds or further declines signal resurgence.

question: Zelenskyy out as Ukraine president by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Volodymyr Zelenskyy ceases to be President of Ukraine for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Volodymyr Zelenskyy's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Volodymyr Zelenskyy and the government of Ukraine; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Zelenskyy out as Ukraine president by March 31, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Volodymyr Zelenskyy ceases to be President of Ukraine for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Volodymyr Zelenskyy's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Volodymyr Zelenskyy and the government of Ukraine; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Zelenskyy remaining Ukraine's president through March 31, 2026, at 99.6% implied probability on "No," driven by constitutional bans on presidential elections during martial law, which parliament has extended multiple times amid Russia's ongoing invasion—most recently into early 2025. With no scheduled vote and Zelenskyy's wartime leadership showing stable public support in polls, traders see negligible risk of removal via resignation, impeachment, or other means before the deadline. Realistic shifts could arise from a surprise ceasefire lifting martial law to enable snap elections, a severe political crisis eroding his coalition, or unforeseen health events, though current frontline stalemates and Western aid flows reinforce continuity.

question: Ukraine election called by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Ukrainian presidential election is scheduled by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is about whether a date for the next Ukrainian election is announced between February 14, 2025, and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Whether the election is supposed to take place during this timeframe or later will have no effect on the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the Government of Ukraine; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Ukraine election called by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Ukrainian presidential election is scheduled in 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is about whether a date for the next Ukrainian election is announced between February 14 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Whether the election is supposed to take place in 2025 or later will have no effect on the resolution to this market.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the Government of Ukraine however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukraine's constitution prohibits presidential elections during martial law, repeatedly extended amid the Russia conflict, blocking any vote until wartime conditions end and parliament lifts restrictions. President Zelenskyy's five-year term technically expired May 20, 2024, but legal experts affirm his continued authority absent elections, fueling opposition claims of illegitimacy. Recent U.S. President-elect Trump's statements demanding Ukrainian polls before further aid have intensified debate, shifting trader consensus toward prolonged delay as Zelenskyy insists votes follow victory. Upcoming parliamentary martial law review in December could signal shifts, though war stalemate keeps "no election soon" odds dominant among bettors wagering on resolution timelines.

question: Will Donald Trump visit Alabama in 2026?
description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits the listed state between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial territory of the listed state. Whether or not Trump enters the state’s airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from the government of the United States of America, official information from Trump, or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g., https://twitter.com/POTUS); however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Which states will Donald Trump visit in 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Donald Trump visiting battleground states like Pennsylvania, Georgia, Michigan, and Wisconsin in 2026, mirroring his 2024 campaign emphasis on swing districts ahead of midterms. These odds reflect historical patterns where Trump prioritized competitive races to boost GOP turnout, with no official 2026 itinerary announced by his transition team as of late 2024. Recent developments include Trump's post-election rallies in key states signaling midterm engagement, while early polling highlights vulnerable Senate seats in Ohio and Montana as potential targets. Upcoming primary filings and special elections could refine probabilities, though presidential duties and unforeseen events add uncertainty to visit predictions.

question: Will LeBron James retire before next NBA season?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if LeBron James retires from the NBA before the start of the 2026-2027 NBA season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An official announcement from James that he is retiring will qualify even if his official retirement has not yet occurred, as long as the announced retirement is stated to take effect before the start of the 2026-2027 NBA season. Announcements that he will retire after the start of the 2026-2027 NBA season will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from LeBron James, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: LeBron James' elite 2023-24 performance—25.7 points and 8.3 assists per game at age 39, plus 27.8 points in the playoffs versus Denver—bolsters the 78.5% trader consensus he won't retire before next season. Holding a $51.9 million player option for 2024-25, James has voiced intent to return, particularly to play alongside Lakers-drafted son Bronny, with no official retirement signals from him or the team. Absent major injuries or roster upheaval, his sustained All-NBA form and longevity track record outweigh age-related speculation driving market odds.

question: Another S&P 500 Company buys Bitcoin by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any S&P 500 company announces between November 4, 12:00 PM ET, and 11:59 PM ET on the date specified in the title that they bought Bitcoin for the first time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This announcement can pertain to a first purchase of Bitcoin outside of the timeframe of this market (e.g. if a qualifying company announces it made its first purchase of Bitcoin prior to this market's timeframe, but the announcement is made within the timeframe, this market will resolve to "Yes").

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from qualifying companies; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Timothy Chalamet confirmed to be EsDeeKid by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if definitive evidence confirming that Timothy Chalamet is EsDeeKid, the anonymous rapper supposedly from Liverpool, England, is made public by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Definitive evidence includes, but is not limited to, official documentation, verified video evidence, or a consensus of credible reporting clearly establishing that Chalamet is EsDeeKid.

Unverified or anonymous claims, speculation, memes, jokes, sarcasm, or unsubstantiated reports will not qualify.

Unsubstantiated claims made by Chalamet or EsDeeKid will not by themselves qualify.

If definitive evidence confirming that EsDeeKid is somebody other than Timothy Chalamet is made public within this market's timeframe, this market will resolve immediately to "No".

The primary resolution sources for this market will be qualifying, publicly-available evidence and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors "No" at 97.1% implied probability that Timothée Chalamet will not be confirmed as the anonymous TikTok sensation EsDeeKid by June 30, driven by the absence of any official statements from Chalamet, his representatives, or EsDeeKid amid persistent online rumors fueled by viral voice comparisons and scheduling speculation. Debunking factors include mismatched vocal timbres—Chalamet's polished French-American inflection versus EsDeeKid's raw, youthful delivery—and Chalamet's packed promo slate for films like Dune: Part Two, clashing with EsDeeKid's posting patterns. With the deadline looming and no credible leaks from entertainment insiders, confidence remains sky-high; a realistic upset would require an unprecedented last-minute reveal, such as a direct social media admission, though historical precedent for celebrity anon-account unmaskings favors skepticism.

question: Pete Hegseth out as Secretary of Defense by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Pete Hegseth ceases to be the US Secretary of Defense for any period of time between December 1, 2025, and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Hegseth's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pete Hegseth's nomination as Secretary of Defense enjoys near-unanimous trader confidence in surviving through March 31, driven by the Republican Senate majority of 53-47 seats and President-elect Trump's unwavering public support amid vetting controversies. Recent developments, including Hegseth's closed-door meetings with key senators like Roger Wicker and Jack Reed, have quelled early Republican defections, with his January 14 Senate Armed Services Committee hearing approaching absent major roadblocks. Traders price in the wisdom of crowds favoring confirmation or recess appointment, reflecting historical base rates for controversial Trump cabinet picks. Realistic shifts could stem from fresh allegations surfacing or unexpected bipartisan opposition during floor votes.

question: Will CDU win the most seats in the 2026 Rhineland-Palatinate parliamentary elections?
description:
Parliamentary elections to elect the Landtag of Rhineland-Palatinate are scheduled to take place in Rhineland-Palatinate on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the formal deliberative assembly of Rhineland-Palatinate (Landtag) as a result of this election.

If voting in the Rhineland-Palatinate election for the Landtag does not occur by July 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid second votes received (Zweitstimme), with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Election Office of Rhineland-Palatinate (Landeswahlleiter Rheinland-Pfalz https://www.wahlen.rlp.de/landtagswahl/ergebnisse)

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices CDU victory in the Rheinland-Pfalz Landtagswahl at virtually 100%, driven by consistent polling leads of 35-40% from surveys like INSA and Forsa, far ahead of SPD (25-28%) and Grüne (12-15%). Incumbent SPD-Grüne coalition faces headwinds from voter frustration over inflation control, energy transition costs, and migration policy enforcement, boosting CDU momentum under state leader Christian Baldauf. Historical CDU strength in the region, including their 2016 win, reinforces this positioning amid stagnant FDP, AfD, and newer BSW support. Realistic challenges include a late scandal hitting CDU, SPD rebound via targeted campaigning, or high turnout favoring left-leaning voters before the March 2026 ballot.

If voting in the Rhineland-Palatinate election for the Landtag does not occur by July 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid second votes received (Zweitstimme), with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Election Office of Rhineland-Palatinate (Landeswahlleiter Rheinland-Pfalz https://www.wahlen.rlp.de/landtagswahl/ergebnisse)

question: Will Laura Gillen be the Democratic nominee for NY-04?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-04 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: NY-04 Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-04 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Laura Gillen's commanding lead in recent polls and fundraising drives her 61.5% trader consensus as NY-04 Democratic primary frontrunner, positioning her strongly against Republican incumbent Anthony D'Esposito in the general election. A June Siena College poll showed Gillen at 44% support, far ahead of Nicholas Sciretta's 13%, with Taylor Darling and Gian Jones trailing in single digits amid low name recognition. Key factors include Gillen's endorsements from Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer, EMILY's List, and local unions, bolstering her moderate appeal in the Nassau County district. Sciretta's 10.5% reflects progressive backing and criticism of Gillen's centrist record, while Darling's 7.8% stems from grassroots efforts; recent debates have slightly narrowed Gillen's margin but reinforced her edge ahead of the June 25 primary.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors state Sen. Matt Little at 59.5% for the MN-02 Democratic primary on August 13, propelled by his lead in the latest Star Tribune/MPR News poll (28% to Matt Klein's 23%) and early DFL party endorsement in April, which bolsters grassroots and union support in the open seat race vacated by Rep. Angie Craig. Klein, at 36.5%, gains from recent fundraising momentum and progressive backing amid attack ads questioning Little's record, while Kaela Berg lingers at 4% with minimal polling at 5%. This tightens sentiment as final voter turnout and any late endorsements loom large.

question: Jang Dong-hyeok out as PPP Leader by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jang Dong-hyeok ceases to be Leader of the People Power Party (PPP) for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Jang Dong-hyeok's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Jang Dong-hyeok and the People Power Party (PPP); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Jang Dong-hyeok and the People Power Party (PPP); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jang Dong-hyeok's landslide election as People Power Party (PPP) leader in mid-2024 has solidified his position, with his two-year term extending well beyond March 31, 2026, driving the 96.6% "No" consensus on Polymarket. Recent party convention results showed overwhelming delegate support amid stable internal dynamics and no major scandals or factional revolts. South Korean political analysts note the PPP's focus on unifying ahead of upcoming legislative sessions and local elections, reducing near-term leadership shakeup risks. Trader confidence reflects this entrenched status, though realistic shifts could arise from a sudden corruption probe, poor by-election outcomes, or intensified Yoon administration tensions prompting a party reset.

question: New pandemic in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the World Health Organization (WHO) declares any disease a pandemic between January 1, 2026 and December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be official announcements from the World Health Organization.

events.title: New pandemic in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the World Health Organization (WHO) declares any disease a pandemic between January 1, 2026 and December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be official announcements from the World Health Organization.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' strong 88% consensus on "No" for a new pandemic in 2026 stems primarily from the absence of any novel pathogen exhibiting sustained human-to-human transmission, as tracked by WHO and CDC surveillance networks bolstered post-COVID. Avian influenza H5N1 persists in animal reservoirs with sporadic human cases—over 60 in the U.S. since 2024—but shows no efficient airborne spread or mutations enabling pandemics, per recent genomic analyses from GISAID. Mpox clades remain regionally contained, while seasonal coronaviruses and influenzas follow predictable baselines. Enhanced global preparedness, including mRNA platforms and wastewater monitoring, further mitigates risks, though experts note zoonotic spillovers carry inherent uncertainty absent clear precursors.

question: Will Elon Musk go on Joe Rogan before June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Elon Musk is a guest on any "The Joe Rogan Experience" podcast episode between December 1, 2025 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If a consensus of credible reporting confirms Elon Musk recorded an episode with Rogan before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of when/if it is released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be Spotify and/or Youtube, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

No confirmed booking exists, but Rogan's guest patterns with tech leaders and Musk's availability post-Tesla earnings suggest alignment, with odds capturing bettors' view of high mutual incentive absent countervailing events.

question: Reya FDV above $1B one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Reya's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Reya (https://x.com/reya_xyz) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Coalition Avenir Québec win the most seats in the 2026 Quebec general election?
description:
Parliamentary elections to elect all 125 seats of the National Assembly of Quebec are scheduled to take place in Quebec on October 5, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the National Assembly of Quebec as a result of this election.

If voting in the 2026 Quebec general election does not occur by January 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie for the most seats won between this party and any other, this market will resolve in favor of the party that won the most valid votes in the respective election. If this also results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation comes first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Élections Québec (https://www.electionsquebec.qc.ca).

Key drivers include voter frustration with CAQ Premier François Legault's handling of housing affordability, healthcare wait times, and immigration policies, boosting PQ's nationalist platform on language protection and sovereignty under leader Paul St-Pierre Plamondon. The Quebec Liberals (PLQ) hold second place at 29% on steady federalist appeal, while CAQ slips to 9.5% as scandals erode its base; minor parties like PCQ, PVQ, and QS trail below 2%. Latest Léger and Abacus-Data surveys through November 2024 confirm PQ's 35-42% lead, with no major events scheduled until potential 2026 vote.

question: Will Thomas Massie be the Republican nominee for KY-04?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the KY-04 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on May 19, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://www.rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: KY-04 Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the KY-04 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on May 19, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://www.rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Thomas Massie's commanding lead in KY-04 Republican primary odds at 63% reflects his entrenched advantages in fundraising, name recognition, and voter loyalty since winning the seat in 2012, bolstered by recent polls showing him ahead 55-65% against challengers. Ed Gallrein's 36.4% share stems from his positioning as a conservative outsider emphasizing fiscal restraint and border security, gaining traction via grassroots support and endorsements from local GOP figures amid criticism of Massie's independent votes on spending bills. Negligible odds for Nicole Lee Ethington and Robert Wells Jr. align with their limited visibility and resources. With the May 21 primary nearing, trader consensus anticipates low turnout favoring Massie's base, though any late Trump endorsement could shift dynamics.

question: Will CDU/CSU–SPD German federal coalition break before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the governing coalition consisting of CDU/CSU and SPD breaks by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM CET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the coalition is considered broken if either CDU/CSU or SPD ceases to be a coalition partner in the federal government.

A coalition break may be evidenced by:
– a formal withdrawal from the coalition,
– the resignation or dismissal of all ministers from one party,
– or the appointment of a new federal government.

If all ministers affiliated with one of the coalition parties resign or are dismissed, this may signal that party’s withdrawal from the coalition, even if one or more individuals remain in office as independents or continue without representing the party.

If the coalition breaks and the sitting Chancellor remains in office with a new coalition or as a minority government, this market will still resolve to “Yes.”

The break date is the date on which it becomes officially confirmed that the coalition has broken; mere reports of negotiations, speculation, or indications of an impending break will not suffice.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the German government; however, a consensus of credible reporting from major reputable news outlets may also be used.

– or the appointment of a new federal government.

The break date is the date on which it becomes officially confirmed that the coalition has broken; mere reports of negotiations, speculation, or indications of an impending break will not suffice.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the German government; however, a consensus of credible reporting from major reputable news outlets may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 83.5% implied probability against a CDU/CSU-SPD grand coalition breaking before 2027, driven by strong polling leads for CDU/CSU ahead of the February 23, 2025 snap election and mutual signals from leaders Friedrich Merz and Lars Klingbeil openness to partnering post-traffic light collapse. Historical grand coalitions under Angela Merkel endured policy disputes without early dissolution, bolstering expectations of stability if formed. Recent developments, including no major inter-party conflicts amid campaign focus on economic recovery and migration, alongside the prior Scholz government's implosion via confidence vote loss in November 2024, underscore traders' view that any new SPD-CDU/CSU pact would prioritize durability through 2027 amid fragmented opposition.

question: Will s1mple retire by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if s1mple, the professional counter-strike player, announces his retirement from professional competitive esports by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement from s1mple that he is retiring will qualify even if his official retirement has not yet occurred.

Breaks, periods of inactivity, transitions to another game, or transitions to streaming will not count unless s1mple explicitly announces his retirement from professional competitive esports. 

The resolution source will be official announcements from s1mple, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans heavily against s1mple retiring by June 30, pricing "No" at 64.7% implied probability, anchored by his high-profile April 2024 return from a two-year competitive hiatus to spearhead Team Falcons in CS2. The 26-year-old rifler has posted dominant form, including a 1.25 HLTV rating and top-8 finish at IEM Dallas, signaling sustained peak performance rather than decline. No injury reports, benchings, or retirement hints from Falcons or his camp disrupt this trajectory; instead, a reported multi-year contract and upcoming major tune-ups like IEM Cologne reinforce roster commitment and momentum, tempering any short-term exit speculation amid esports' grueling schedule.

question: Will CDU win the most seats in the 2026 Berlin state elections?
description:
Parliamentary elections to elect the Abgeordnetehaus of Berlin are scheduled to take place in Berlin on September 20, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the formal deliberative assembly of Berlin (Abgeordnetenhaus) as a result of this election.

If voting in the Berlin election for the Abgeordnetenhaus does not occur by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the case of a tie between this party or coalition and any other for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation comes first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Election Office of Berlin (Landeswahlleiter Berlin https://www.berlin.de/wahlen/)

events.title: Berlin State Election Winner
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions the CDU as the clear frontrunner to win the most seats in Berlin's next state election, with 54.5% implied probability, reflecting its commanding 30-35% support in the latest opinion polls from INSA and Forschunggruppe Wahlen. CDU gains stem from voter frustration over the SPD-Green coalition's struggles with chronic public transport failures, housing shortages, and budget overruns, amplifying appeals on law-and-order and fiscal discipline under Governing Mayor Kai Wegner. AfD's 13.6% odds capture rising anti-migration sentiment, while Die Linke's 10% holds firm among protest voters. SPD and Greens trail below 7% amid incumbency wear and recent scandals, including a June 2024 senate embezzlement probe. A September poll widened CDU's lead to 34%, though multi-party dynamics and potential coalitions add uncertainty ahead of the 2029 vote.

question: Nocries signs for a pro CS2 organization by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if nocries signs with a professional Counter-Strike 2 organization by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A professional Counter-Strike 2 organization is any organization that fields a paid Counter-Strike 2 roster in official CS2 competitions.

“Signs” refers to nocries entering into a paid contract with an organization to compete in Counter-Strike 2 for that organization. Trials or other relationships that do not include a signed professional playing contract will not count. Sponsorship agreements or other contracts that do not pay nocries to compete in Counter-Strike 2 for a specific organization will also not count.

A signed professional contract between nocries and a qualifying Counter-Strike 2 organization within this market’s timeframe will qualify even if its effective start date is after June 30, 2026.

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from the team involved and nocries, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With June 30 fast approaching, the absence of any official announcement or credible reports of Nocries joining a Tier 1 CS2 organization anchors the 67.5% implied probability on "No," reflecting trader consensus on stalled recruitment talks. The 16-year-old French rifler's strong academy form—highlighted by Karmine Corp's BLAST Rivals Spring win—has fueled speculation, but pro teams remain cautious amid age-related visa hurdles, travel logistics, and preference for established 18+ talent. Free agency dynamics show no trial confirmations or agent leaks from beat sources like HLTV, while past rumors of interest from orgs like Vitality fizzled without action, underscoring the high bar for rapid pro elevation in a crowded CS2 roster market.

question: Mamdani removes Jessica Tisch as NYC Police Commissioner by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jessica Tisch ceases to be the New York City Police Commissioner for any period of time between December 2, 2025 and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Tisch's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect. An announcement from New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani that he will remove Tisch from her post will qualify. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York City local government or Police Department; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Mamdani removes Jessica Tisch as NYC Police Commissioner by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Jessica Tisch ceases to be the New York City Police Commissioner for any period of time between December 2, 2025 and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Tisch's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect. An announcement from New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani that he will remove Tisch from her post will qualify. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York City local government or Police Department; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: NYC Councilmember Zohran Mamdani lacks authority to unilaterally remove Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch, whose tenure depends on Mayor Eric Adams, driving the 96% "No" trader consensus by March 31, 2025. Mamdani's October 30 non-binding resolution criticizing Tisch's handling of campus protests and demonstrations has stalled without council support or vote, while Adams has reaffirmed her leadership amid NYPD reform debates. This symbolic gesture reflects limited political momentum against Tisch's 2023 appointment. Realistic catalysts for reversal include a major scandal implicating Tisch or Adams' potential resignation from federal investigations, though current stability and no scheduled actions suggest sustained high confidence in continuation.

question: 2026 U.S. House election: Republicans flip the Democrats by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, between the Republican Party and the Democratic Party, the Republican Party becomes the favorite in the "Which party will win the House in 2026?" market (https://polymarket.com/event/which-party-will-win-the-house-in-2026?tid=1764045739181) for any four-hour period ending by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or earlier. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The Republican Party will be considered to have become the favorite for any four-hour period if it is ahead of the Democratic Party in more individual minutes than the Democratic Party is ahead of the Republican Party during a qualifying time period.

A tie in the number of minutes will not qualify.

The resolution source will be the underlying minute-level price data for the “Which party will win the House in 2026?” market, which can be viewed using the bar chart on https://derivative.polymarket.com/index/will-the-republicans-flip-the-democrats-for-the-2026-us-house-elections-by or through the “Resolution” tab with the time set to show the four-hour window. Both interfaces display the same underlying data.

Note: This market will resolve according to the price scoring methodology of derivative.polymarket.com.

eventMetadata.context_description: Republicans' narrow 220-215 House majority after the 2024 elections forms the baseline for 2026 midterm odds, where historical precedent shows the president's party—here Republicans under Trump—losing an average of 26 seats. Trader consensus reflects caution on major GOP net gains from Democrats, prioritizing factors like economic performance, Trump's approval ratings, and early retirement announcements from vulnerable GOP incumbents in swing districts. Recent special election results, such as Democratic holds in blue-leaning seats, reinforce Democratic resilience. Key upcoming catalysts include 2026 primaries starting mid-2025 and generic ballot polling shifts, which could signal voter enthusiasm amid legislative battles over budget and immigration.

question: Will Cameron Boozer win the 2025-2026 Naismith Men's College Player of the Year Award?
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the Naismith College Player of the Year Award for the 2025-2026 Men’s NCAA division 1 basketball season.

If the listed player is not included as a semifinalist or finalist for the Naismith College Player of the Year Award, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no winner is announced by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary source of resolution for this market will be official information from the Naismith Awards organizers (https://naismithtrophy.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: 2025-2026 Naismith College Player of the Year
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the Naismith College Player of the Year Award for the 2025-2026 Men’s NCAA division 1 basketball season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cameron Boozer's dominant 95.2% implied probability in the 2025-2026 Naismith College Player of the Year market stems from his status as the top-ranked college-bound prospect in the 2025 class, fresh off a Duke commitment that positions him for immediate stardom on a national title contender under coach Jon Scheyer. His high school dominance—33+ PPG and double-double rebounding averages at Christopher Columbus—combined with Duke's track record elevating freshmen like Zion Williamson, fuels trader consensus on his stat-stuffing potential amid a loaded Blue Devils roster. Challenges could arise if AJ Dybantsa erupts at BYU for a surprise Big 12 run, Boozer suffers an early injury, or Duke underperforms in the loaded ACC, opening doors for dark horses like Yaxel Lendeborg at Miami.

This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the Naismith College Player of the Year Award for the 2025-2026 Men’s NCAA division 1 basketball season.

If the listed player is not included as a semifinalist or finalist for the Naismith College Player of the Year Award, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no winner is announced by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary source of resolution for this market will be official information from the Naismith Awards organizers (https://naismithtrophy.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cameron Boozer's dominant 95.2% implied probability in the 2025-2026 Naismith College Player of the Year market stems from his status as the top-ranked college-bound prospect in the 2025 class, fresh off a Duke commitment that positions him for immediate stardom on a national title contender under coach Jon Scheyer. His high school dominance—33+ PPG and double-double rebounding averages at Christopher Columbus—combined with Duke's track record elevating freshmen like Zion Williamson, fuels trader consensus on his stat-stuffing potential amid a loaded Blue Devils roster. Challenges could arise if AJ Dybantsa erupts at BYU for a surprise Big 12 run, Boozer suffers an early injury, or Duke underperforms in the loaded ACC, opening doors for dark horses like Yaxel Lendeborg at Miami.

question: Will Royce White be the Republican nominee for Senate in Minnesota?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Minnesota.

If no 2026 Minnesota Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Minnesota Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Minnesota Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Minnesota.

If no 2026 Minnesota Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Minnesota Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michele Tafoya leads Minnesota Republican Senate primary trader consensus at 74.5% following her high-profile campaign launch on July 30, 2024, leveraging national name recognition from ESPN and conservative media appearances to challenge Democratic incumbent Tina Smith in 2026. Adam Schwarze holds second at 11.8%, buoyed by grassroots fundraising and local endorsements, while Raymond Petersen's 5.9% reflects steady volunteer momentum. Royce White trails at 4.1% amid past primary controversies. With no public polls yet and the August 2026 primary distant, odds hinge on early fundraising reports due next week and potential field consolidations, underscoring traders' focus on visibility over established records.

eventMetadata.context_description: Peggy Flanagan's commanding 79% trader consensus in the Minnesota Democratic U.S. Senate primary market reflects her status as Lieutenant Governor and early frontrunner following Tina Smith's February 2025 retirement announcement, opening the 2026 race. Recent polling from SurveyUSA and internal Democratic surveys shows Flanagan leading with strong progressive credentials and statewide name recognition, bolstered by her campaign launch and robust fundraising exceeding $2 million. Angie Craig holds 17.5% as a centrist U.S. Representative weighing a bid, while trailing candidates like Keith Ellison, Ilhan Omar, and Melisa López Franzen face re-election priorities or lower visibility. Odds highlight trader bets on Flanagan's momentum ahead of the August 2026 primary, though the field could shift with new entrants.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns an 86.6% implied probability to Alexander Vindman winning the Florida Democratic U.S. Senate primary, dwarfing U.S. Rep. Jared Moskowitz at 5.5% and trailing figures like Jennifer Jenkins, Angie Nixon, Alan Grayson, Josh Weil, Charlie Crist, and Joey Atkins below 1% each. Vindman's lead stems from his national recognition as the retired Army lieutenant colonel who testified during Donald Trump's first impeachment inquiry and his Florida residency, fueling speculation on a potential late entry despite the June 14 candidate qualifying deadline passing without these names on official filings. Absent new announcements or polls elevating filed contenders like Debbie Mucarsel-Powell—who leads early surveys—the lopsided odds reflect bettor focus on profile over formal status ahead of the August 20 primary.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sherrod Brown's dominant 96.7% trader consensus in the Ohio Democratic Senate primary reflects his entrenched incumbency as a three-term Senator with unmatched name recognition, fundraising superiority, and a track record of narrow wins in a competitive state. Post-filing deadline in late 2023, challengers like Rep. Greg Landsman, state Rep. Allison Russo, and ex-Rep. Tim Ryan remain fringe figures with negligible polling or campaign momentum, underscoring minimal intra-party competition ahead of the March 19, 2024, primary. Scenarios to upend this include a sudden Brown scandal or unexpected heavyweight entrant, though deadlines and time constraints make such disruptions improbable.

question: Will the United Hearts Movement win the most seats in the 2025 Central African Republic National Assembly election?
description:
The 2025 Central African Republic general election is scheduled to be held on December 28, 2025, to elect the 140 members of the National Assembly (CAR’s unicameral parliament).

This market will resolve according to the party that controls the most seats in the Central African Republic National Assembly as a result of the next Central African parliamentary election.

If initial voting in the next Central African parliamentary election does not occur by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market may remain open to consider any second-round votes held to determine winners in individual single-member constituencies, as long as the remaining undecided seats can still change which party wins the most seats overall, or maximally until December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, after which the market will resolve based on the results available at that time.

In the case of a tie between this party and any other for the most seats held, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation comes first in alphabetical order.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by this party, not any coalition of which it may be a part.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Government of the Central African Republic, specifically the National Elections Authority (https://www.ane-rca.org/).

question: Will the Union for Central African Renewal win the most seats in the 2025 Central African Republic National Assembly election?
description:

This market will resolve according to the party that controls the most seats in the Central African Republic National Assembly as a result of the next Central African parliamentary election.

This market may remain open to consider any second-round votes held to determine winners in individual single-member constituencies, as long as the remaining undecided seats can still change which party wins the most seats overall, or maximally until December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, after which the market will resolve based on the results available at that time.

In the case of a tie between this party and any other for the most seats held, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation comes first in alphabetical order.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. question: Will Google have the top AI model at the end of March 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model with the highest arena score based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on March 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Arena Score" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text set to default (style control on) will be used to resolve this market.

If two models are tied for the best arena score at this market's check time, resolution will be based on whichever company's name, as it is described in this market group, comes first in alphabetical order (e.g., if both were tied, "Google" would resolve to "Yes", and "xAI" would resolve to "No")

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolves based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

events.title: Which company has the top AI model end of March? (Style Control On)
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model with the highest arena score based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on March 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolves based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic's commanding 94.3% implied probability stems from its Claude 3 Opus model holding the top position on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard in the Style Control On category as of late March, following its March 4 release that showcased superior reasoning and creative control over rivals like GPT-4 Turbo and Gemini 1.5 Pro. Traders cite consistent benchmark dominance—Elo scores above 1280—and the absence of announced challengers from OpenAI, Google, or xAI by month-end as key supports, with historical precedent showing leaderboard stability post-major launches. Realistic threats include an unheralded release from DeepSeek or Mistral, or a sudden OpenAI update, though tight timelines make these low-probability events.

question: Will Mark Warner be the Democratic nominee for Senate in Virginia?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Virginia.

If no 2026 Virginia Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Virginia Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Virginia Democratic Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Virginia.

If no 2026 Virginia Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Virginia Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent U.S. Senator Mark Warner's dominant 92.5% trader consensus in the Virginia Democratic Senate primary reflects his entrenched incumbency advantage, massive fundraising edge exceeding $20 million, and scant viable opposition from challenger Jason Reynolds, a low-profile pastor with minimal campaign infrastructure. Warner's long tenure, cross-party appeal in purple Virginia, and recent polling leads of 70+ points underscore trader confidence ahead of the June 18 primary. While Reynolds garners 3.5% on niche anti-establishment sentiment, realistic disruptions—like a sudden Warner scandal, viral challenger endorsement, or depressed turnout—remain improbable given historical incumbent win rates over 90% in uncontested races.

Results from the "Arena Score" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text set to default (style control on) will be used to resolve this market.

If two models are tied for the second-highest arena score at this market's check time, resolution will be based on whichever company's name, as it is described in this market group, comes first in alphabetical order (e.g., if both were tied, "Google" would resolve to "Yes", and "xAI" would resolve to "No")

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolves based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Anthropic at 93.6% implied probability for the #2 AI model spot on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard (Style Control On) by end of March, propelled by Claude 3.5 Sonnet's sustained top-tier Elo scores—currently leading overall—while Claude 3 Opus anchors a robust second place amid minimal leaderboard flux. Recent benchmarks confirm Claude's edge in style-controlled evaluations, where nuanced instruction-following and creative output excel, outpacing rivals like OpenAI's GPT-4o variants. Challenges could arise from xAI's Grok-2 beta surprises, Google's Gemini 2.0 previews, or DeepSeek's rapid iterations, but no confirmed launches threaten displacement before resolution, reinforcing trader confidence in Anthropic's positioning.

question: Will Alexander Gaasserud be the Republican nominee for Senate in West Virginia?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from West Virginia.

If no 2026 West Virginia Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the West Virginia Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: West Virginia Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from West Virginia.

If no 2026 West Virginia Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the West Virginia Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent U.S. Senator Shelley Moore Capito's commanding 95.9% implied probability in the West Virginia Republican Senate primary stems from her strong track record, including a 2020 primary win over Tom Willis by nearly 60 points, robust fundraising, and high name recognition in a deeply conservative state. Trader consensus reflects her incumbency advantage and lack of viable challengers, with Willis at 3.5% after previous underperformance and Alexander Gaasserud at 0.4% as a fringe contender. Realistic challenges could arise from Capito opting not to run again, a major scandal, or a high-profile endorsement shifting momentum ahead of the 2026 filing deadline.

eventMetadata.context_description: Google leads trader consensus at 61% implied probability for holding the #3 AI model spot by March's end on the LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard (Style Control On category), driven by Gemini 2.0 Flash's recent surge to top-three contention via enhanced reasoning and multimodal capabilities in official benchmarks. xAI trails at 26% amid hype for Grok-3's imminent release, following Elon Musk's announcements of completed training on massive Memphis Supercluster compute, potentially disrupting rankings with superior long-context performance. Anthropic's 5.3% reflects Claude 3.5 Sonnet's lock on #1 but vulnerability in style-aligned evals; lower odds for DeepSeek and others stem from inconsistent leaderboard climbs despite cost-efficient releases. Key watch: xAI launch timing and Google's I/O previews could swing odds before resolution.
